_README: Niche vocabulary so the (offline) matcher UNDERSTANDS jargon and descriptions INCLUDE it. Per term: aka=synonyms, visual=what it looks like on screen (cue phrases used to expand text before embedding), label=related action label(s). Sources: CandleScience, Nature's Garden, NorthWood Candle & Craft, NI Candle Supplies, Kalamazoo Candle glossaries. '_common' applies to all niches. Keep in sync with YT-Clip-Classifier config/vocab.yaml.

tunneling.visual: the wax burns straight down the middle around the wick leaving a ring of thick hard unmelted wax up the sides of the jar; a deep hole or core down the centre, a narrow melt pool
label: reveal_result
cure_cool

memory_burn.visual: the critical first burn melting the wax all the way out to the edges of the container to set the melt pool boundary
label: reveal_result

borehole.visual: a narrow deep hole bored straight down into the wax around the wick
label: reveal_result

sinkhole.visual: a dip, pit, cavity or collapsed void that forms in the top of the candle as the wax cools and contracts, often around the wick
label: cure_cool
reveal_result

relief_holes.visual: poking holes into the cooling wax around the wick with a skewer to release trapped air and prevent sinkholes
label: cure_cool
set_wick

repour.visual: pouring a second layer of melted wax on top to fill in shrinkage, sinkholes or an uneven set surface
label: pour_wax
decorate_finish

wet_spots.visual: patches where the wax has pulled away from the glass jar, looking like wet translucent or lighter marks against the side of the container
label: cure_cool
reveal_result

frosting.visual: a white crystalline frosty bloom or coating growing on the surface or sides of soy or vegetable wax
label: reveal_result
cure_cool

jump_lines.visual: horizontal lines, seams or ridges across the side of a container or pillar candle where the wax set too quickly while pouring
label: reveal_result
pour_wax

mushrooming.visual: a bulbous black carbon ball building up on the tip of the burning wick like a mushroom head, with a large flame
label: reveal_result
trim_wick

sooting.visual: black smoke and soot coming off the flame, leaving black marks up the inside of the glass
label: reveal_result

drowning_out.visual: the flame shrinking and the wick being submerged or swamped by a deep pool of melted wax
label: reveal_result

hang_up.visual: a rim or ledge of unmelted wax left clinging to the container wall after burning
label: reveal_result

sweating.visual: droplets or beads of oil seeping out and pooling on the surface of the finished wax
label: reveal_result
cure_cool

melt_pool.visual: the pool of liquefied wax across the surface around the lit wick while the candle burns
label: reveal_result

seizing.visual: the wax suddenly thickening, clumping or going grainy into a paste when fragrance or dye is stirred in too cold
label: add_fragrance
add_dye_color

smooth_tops.visual: a flat glossy glass-like even surface on the finished candle with no lumps or holes
label: reveal_result
cure_cool

double_boiler.visual: wax melting in a metal pouring pitcher or jug set inside a pot of simmering water on the stove
label: melt_wax

pour_temperature.visual: a thermometer reading the temperature of the melted wax before adding fragrance or pouring
label: monitor_temperature
measure_wax
pour_wax

flashpoint.visual: the temperature at which the fragrance oil's vapour could ignite, checked on a thermometer before adding scent
label: monitor_temperature
add_fragrance

fragrance_load.visual: weighing the fragrance oil to a set percentage of the wax weight on a scale before stirring it in
label: add_fragrance
measure_wax

cold_throw.visual: smelling the unlit finished candle to judge its scent before burning
label: add_fragrance
reveal_result

hot_throw.visual: the lit candle filling the room with fragrance, scent strength while burning
label: reveal_result

curing.visual: finished candles left untouched for days to harden and let the fragrance bind into the wax
label: cure_cool

wick_priming.visual: a wax-coated wick with a metal sustainer tab being stuck to the bottom centre of the container
label: set_wick
prepare_container

wick_bar.visual: a bar, stick, clip or clothespin laid across the jar rim holding the wick centred and upright while the wax sets
label: set_wick

wick_trimming.visual: trimming the burnt wick down to about a quarter inch with wick trimmers or scissors
label: trim_wick
decorate_finish

wood_wick.visual: a flat wooden wick in the candle that crackles like a fireplace when lit
label: set_wick
reveal_result

double_wicking.visual: setting two or more wicks in a wide container so the whole surface melts evenly
label: set_wick

stearic_acid.visual: a white powdery hardening additive being stirred into the melted wax to firm up pillar candles
label: add_dye_color
melt_wax

titanium_dioxide.visual: a white pigment added to opacify and whiten the wax
label: add_dye_color

mica_powder.visual: shimmery pearlescent mica powder being brushed or stirred on for sparkle and colour
label: add_dye_color
decorate_finish

candle_dye.visual: concentrated candle dye blocks, chips or liquid colourant being melted into the wax to tint it
label: add_dye_color

container_candle.visual: a candle poured directly into a glass jar, tin or ceramic vessel
label: prepare_container
pour_wax

pillar_candle.visual: a freestanding moulded candle released from a mould, no container
label: reveal_result
prepare_container

votive.visual: a small short candle about two inches tall poured in a little cup
label: reveal_result

tealight.visual: tiny shallow candles in small metal or plastic cups
label: reveal_result
pour_wax

wax_melt.visual: wickless scented wax cubes or shapes popped out of a mould to melt in a warmer
label: reveal_result
decorate_finish

b_roll.visual: close-up footage of the action or object, no presenter talking to camera
